resources.string: Learn to make voiced sounds using tenten and maru.

Okay, get this. You actually don't have to learn a bunch of brand new characters right now. We can just upgrade the ones you already know! 

See these two little dashes? We call them "tenten". Think of them as a harder first letter. When you add them, the sound gets harder and you get the next row. It's so easy to remember once you hear it. 

And then there's this little circle, the "maru". It looks exactly like a tiny bubble, right? It's a special little rule that only works on the H-row to give it a popping sound.

Here is the neat trick to remembering all of these. Think of the tenten marks as just a "harder" version of the first letter like "sa" to "za": 

- K becomes G 
- S becomes Z 
- T becomes D 
- H becomes B 

Now for that little maru bubble. Just remember that bubbles "pop" as in the letter "p"
The G-Row (K gets heavier) \nが ga | ぎ gi | ぐ gu | げ ge | ご go
The Z-Row (S gets heavier) \nざ za | じ ji | ず zu | ぜ ze | ぞ zo \nTip: Notice that \"shi\" becomes \"ji\".
The D-Row (T gets heavier) \nだ da | ぢ ji | づ zu | で de | ど do \nTip: \"chi\" and \"tsu\" also become \"ji\" and \"zu\" here, but they are rarely used!
The B-Row (H gets heavier) \nば ba | び bi | ぶ bu | べ be | ぼ bo
The P-Row (H goes pop!) \nぱ pa | ぴ pi | ぷ pu | ぺ pe | ぽ po
